fields.label: Team ID
fields.description: The ID of the Vercel team to query.

fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Vercel Access Tokens are required to authenticate and use the Vercel API. Tokens are either scoped to your full account or a specific team. If a token is scoped to a team, you must also provide a team ID that matches the scope of the token.

fields.label: Token
fields.description: Token for accessing the datasource API

groups.title: Connection
groups.description: Provide information to connect to the data source

instructions.msg: Vercel configuration is stored under a service-keyed jsonData shape: jsonData.services.vercel.* holds per-service settings and jsonData.variables.<name> holds connection variables, while secrets use flat dotted secureJsonData keys of the form "vercel.<secret>". There is a single service with id 'vercel'.

Authentication uses a Vercel Access Token (bearer). jsonData.services.vercel.auth.id is 'vercelApiKey' (the backend defaults to it) and the token is the write-only secret at secureJsonData key "vercel.token".

Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{"services":{"vercel":{"auth":{"id":"vercelApiKey"}}}},"secureJsonData":{"vercel.token":"<vercel-access-token>"}}. If the token is scoped to a team, also set jsonData.variables.team_id to that team's ID.
Connection: the server URL is fixed at https://api.vercel.com (no server selector). jsonData.variables.team_id is optional and only needed for team-scoped tokens; it is not part of the base URL.

The secret at secureJsonData key "vercel.token" is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether it is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields["vercel.token"] == true rather than secureJsonData.
